parsed_review.summary: CP00-162 adds descriptor-only Master Data permission/audit binding descriptors, contract v0.7 parity checks, tests, H04/C04 packets, and CP00-163 handoff. Claude found no P0/P1/P2 blockers. Three P3 items were fixed and one P3 was explicitly deferred as a safe no-error-state hardening note for later packs.

boundary_assessment.notes: CP00-162 builds permission/audit display descriptors only; package code does not execute runtime permission checks, audit appends, API handlers, UI rendering, Hermes, or Claude.

findings.title: Test/validator depend on the live plan entry; manifest fallback is unpopulated
findings.evidence: cp162PlanPack resolves via closeoutPlan.packs.find(... 'CP00-162') ?? cp162Manifest?.plan_binding_snapshot. The plan currently contains CP00-162 (verified: 10 units S06-S15, deliverable split implementation=3/security_audit=2/ui=4/claude_review=1, all RP04), so validation passes. However docs/closeout-packs/cp00-162/manifest.json does not exist (no pack cp00-15x/cp00-16x has a manifest dir), so the fallback is a no-op. If the plan is ever pruned of closed packs, coverage validation here and in the contract script would silently flip to valid=false. Non-blocking today.
findings.recommended_action: Either keep the closed pack in the plan permanently or emit a cp00-162 manifest with plan_binding_snapshot so the documented fallback is real.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: CP00-162 manifest now carries plan_binding_snapshot, so the documented fallback is real after this closeout artifact is created.

findings.title: Prohibited-output guard scans only customer_facing_descriptor via substring match
findings.evidence: prohibited_outputs_absent is computed as prohibited_outputs.every(v => !JSON.stringify(customer_facing_descriptor).includes(v)). The broader returned object exposes internal binding vocabulary (binding_states like 'review_badge_visible_without_dispatch', interaction_contract, layout_contract) at top level, outside the scanned subtree. None of those strings are in the prohibited list and this pack performs no rendering, so it is safe; but the safe surface is specifically customer_facing_descriptor, not the whole result.
findings.recommended_action: Document (or enforce downstream) that only customer_facing_descriptor is renderable, so a future UI binding does not surface the internal descriptor vocabulary carried alongside it.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: permission/audit binding descriptors now declare renderable_surface=customer_facing_descriptor_only and catalog/contract/validator parity checks preserve that boundary.

findings.title: Contract validator omits parity checks for two descriptor sub-fields
findings.evidence: The script asserts nearly every permission_audit_binding field but does not assert safe_error_contract.source ('safe_error_code_only') nor the permission_audit_binding.binding pack-level pack_id (the contract block carries binding_id only, not pack_id). Both values are present and correct in contract+registry, so there is no current drift; this is only a gap in drift detection.
findings.recommended_action: Add requireEqual checks for permission_audit_binding.safe_error_contract.source to close the parity gap, consistent with the other looped field assertions.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: contract and validator now assert permission_audit_binding.pack_id, renderable_surface, and safe_error_contract.source parity in addition to the existing arrays and state checks.

findings.title: safe_error_code resolves to null for non-enumerated scenarios
findings.evidence: When both permission_ref and audit_hint_ref are present and scenario is neither 'denied' nor 'review_required' (e.g. an arbitrary caller passing scenario='primary_interaction'), safeErrorCode falls through to null and error_message_copy.message becomes 'Descriptor references are linked.'. This is a safe no-error state and is not exercised by the four catalog fixtures or the three tests (all of which yield allowed codes), so it is correct as-is. Note the catalog's safe_error_copy_only check would reject a null code, but no built fixture hits that path.
findings.recommended_action: No change required for this descriptor pack; optionally constrain scenario to the enumerated set if arbitrary external callers are anticipated in a later pack.
findings.post_review_disposition: deferred: non-enumerated scenarios remain a safe no-error descriptor state; catalog fixtures and production-gated validators exercise only enumerated review, denied, permission-missing, and audit-missing states.
